description: RD-Agent(Q) Slack-driven quant research and paper trading system - standalone repo; Slack orchestrator (Claude) drives RD-Agent(Q) research loops via server_ui, records to Notion, and a deterministic nightly rebalancer trades an Alpaca PAPER account through the OneCLI gateway. Live trading is out of scope. Source PRD: tasks/prd-rdagent-q-trading.md; architecture: PLAN.md.

userStories.title: Repo scaffold with lint/typecheck/test tooling
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the repo skeleton and quality tooling so every later story has a stable, checkable base.
acceptanceCriteria: Directories exist: orchestrator/, execution/, research/, data/, ops/, docs/reference/, tests/
pyproject.toml with Python >=3.10, pytest, and pyright configured (the 'Typecheck passes' criterion in every story means pyright, this is a Python repo); ruff configured
README.md states the standalone constraint (no nanoclaw dependency; OneCLI at http://127.0.0.1:10254 is the only external service) and links PLAN.md and tasks/prd-rdagent-q-trading.md
A `make check` (or equivalent script) runs lint + typecheck + pytest and exits 0 on the empty scaffold
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Pin and install RD-Agent
userStories.description: As a developer, I want RD-Agent installed at a pinned commit so research code has a stable upstream.
acceptanceCriteria: research/PINNED_COMMIT contains the chosen microsoft/RD-Agent commit SHA
Install path documented and scripted (research/install.sh or pyproject extra) installing rdagent from that pinned commit into the project venv
python -c 'import rdagent' exits 0 in the venv
docs/decisions.md records the pinned commit and why
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: OneCLI identity setup and verification scripts
userStories.description: As a developer, I want scripted OneCLI identity registration and verification so credential scoping is reproducible and provable.
acceptanceCriteria: ops/setup_onecli.sh registers identities rdq-orchestrator, rdq-research, rdq-exec-paper against http://127.0.0.1:10254 (idempotent; NO rdq-exec-live)
ops/check_onecli.sh makes one bare proxied request per assigned service per identity and reports pass/fail per check; exits nonzero listing missing secret assignments when a check fails
check_onecli.sh asserts rdq-exec-paper gets 401/403 from https://api.alpaca.markets (live-host isolation proof)
Both scripts pass shellcheck
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: LLM backend probe (Claude via LiteLLM + embeddings)
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a scripted probe of RD-Agent's LLM path so the Claude-via-LiteLLM decision is verified before anything depends on it.
acceptanceCriteria: research/.env.example sets CHAT_MODEL=anthropic/claude-sonnet-5, EMBEDDING_MODEL=voyage/voyage-3.5-lite, and placeholder ANTHROPIC_API_KEY + VOYAGE_API_KEY (real values injected by OneCLI proxy; no OpenAI variables anywhere)
research/probe_llm.py sends a JSON-mode hypothesis-shaped prompt through LiteLLM and validates the response against a JSON schema; exits 0 on success
probe_llm.py also performs one Voyage embedding call (voyage/voyage-3.5-lite via LiteLLM) end-to-end through the proxy and asserts a non-empty vector
Probe outcome (Claude confirmed, or documented fallback to another LiteLLM-supported chat provider with the failing behavior) recorded in docs/decisions.md
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: DONE 2026-07-08: Claude via LiteLLM CONFIRMED (no fallback needed). probe_llm.py exits 0 through the proxy: schema-valid JSON hypothesis from anthropic/claude-sonnet-5 + 1024-dim Voyage vector. Gotchas recorded in docs/decisions.md and research/CLAUDE.md (temperature must be omitted for claude-sonnet-5; JSON mode may emit ```json fences).

userStories.title: Vanilla RD-Agent environment check and run wrapper
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the vanilla fin_factor run scripted with a fast environment check so the engine is proven runnable on this box.
acceptanceCriteria: ops/run_vanilla_factor.sh wraps `onecli run --agent rdq-research -- rdagent fin_factor --loop_n 1` with LOG_TRACE_PATH/WORKSPACE_PATH set
`rdagent health_check` exits 0 (Docker sudo-less, ports free) when invoked via the script's --check flag
Script header documents that the first full run downloads cn_data and builds local_qlib:latest (hours); full-run completion criterion: qlib_res.csv with non-null IC exists in the run workspace
--check mode verified end-to-end (run it and record output in progress notes)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: DONE 2026-07-08: --check verified end-to-end (health_check + hard asserts, exit 0). PREREQ FIX: rdagent CLI was broken by unpinned pydantic-ai-slim 2.x; install.sh now pins 1.107.0 (docs/decisions.md). health_check env leg skipped by design (upstream crashes on Anthropic+Voyage env; probe_llm.py covers LLM). Full multi-hour fin_factor run NOT executed this iteration — wrapper ready, completion criterion in script header.

userStories.title: Slack Bolt Socket Mode skeleton
userStories.description: As an operator, I want a bot connected to #quant-research over Socket Mode so I can talk to the system with no inbound port.
acceptanceCriteria: orchestrator/app.py starts a Bolt-for-Python Socket Mode app; channel and thread messages reach a handler; handler can reply in-thread

Token source DECIDED: repo-local .env fallback (chat tokens only, not brokerage keys — acceptable per PLAN.md). Read SLACK_OAUTH_TOKEN (xoxb- bot token) and SLACK_SOCKET_TOKEN (xapp- app-level token) from the repo-root .env; .env is gitignored and must never be committed. Do NOT route Slack through the OneCLI proxy. Record the decision and rationale in docs/decisions.md
Config loading (channel id, token source) lives in orchestrator/config.py; loads the two tokens from .env and the target channel from SLACK_CHANNEL_ID (see story notes) with a clear error if unset
Unit tests with a mocked Slack client cover message routing (channel message -> handler, thread reply targets thread_ts)
Tests pass
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: DONE 2026-07-08: config.py (.env fallback, ConfigError naming missing vars) + app.py (Socket Mode skeleton, in-thread ack handler) + 15 unit tests routing real BoltRequests through App.dispatch() with a mocked WebClient. Live Socket Mode handshake verified with the real tokens ('Bolt app is running!', session established). STILL NEEDED from human before the bot is usable in-channel: SLACK_CHANNEL_ID in .env, bot invited to #quant-research, scopes chat:write + channels:history + reactions:write (+files:write for US-022). Bolt testing gotchas in orchestrator/CLAUDE.md.

userStories.title: Orchestrator SQLite state layer
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a small state store so thread-to-run mappings and the promoted strategy survive restarts.
acceptanceCriteria: orchestrator/state.py creates orchestrator/state.sqlite on startup with tables: directives, runs (thread_ts, session_path, status, universe), promoted_strategy (single row), pending_interactions
Schema migration runs idempotently on startup (running twice is safe)
CRUD helpers for each table with unit tests (create, fetch by thread_ts, replace promoted strategy)
Tests pass
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: DONE 2026-07-08: orchestrator/state.py StateStore — idempotent migration (CREATE IF NOT EXISTS, reruns every startup), per-call connections (thread-safe to share one instance), typed dataclass rows. Dedup in schema: runs.thread_ts PK raises DuplicateRunError (US-020); pending_interactions.interaction_key UNIQUE returns None on dup (US-021); promoted_strategy CHECK(id=1) upsert (US-033). 20 unit tests incl. restart survival; default-path startup smoke-verified.

userStories.title: Central model router and Claude client wrapper
userStories.description: As a developer, I want model selection centralized so the tier policy (fable=judgment, haiku=utility) is enforced in one place.
acceptanceCriteria: orchestrator/llm.py exposes judgment() and utility() call paths; judgment uses model claude-fable-5, utility uses claude-haiku-4-5; model IDs appear only in this module/config

judgment path sends betas=['server-side-fallback-2026-06-01'] with fallbacks=[{'model': 'claude-opus-4-8'}], omits the thinking parameter, uses streaming, and checks stop_reason=='refusal' before reading content
Tool-use loop helper executes registered tools and feeds tool_result blocks back until end_turn
Unit tests with a mocked Anthropic client cover: model selection per path, refusal handling branch, tool loop termination
Tests pass
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: DONE 2026-07-08: orchestrator/llm.py ModelRouter — judgment() streams claude-fable-5 with betas=[server-side-fallback-2026-06-01] + fallbacks=[claude-opus-4-8], no thinking param, RefusalError raised before content is read; utility() = claude-haiku-4-5; judgment_tool_loop() executes ToolSpec handlers until end_turn (handler errors -> is_error tool_result). 13 unit tests incl. a grep test enforcing model IDs live only in llm.py. LIVE-verified through OneCLI proxy: all three paths returned end_turn (fable-5 access + fallback beta confirmed working with the vaulted key).

userStories.title: Conversational core with save_directive tool
userStories.description: As an operator, I want the bot to refine my raw idea into a structured research directive it persists and echoes back.
acceptanceCriteria: System prompt (quant research desk PM: honest reporting, never trades without explicit approval) lives in orchestrator/prompts.py
save_directive tool persists {objective, universe_hint, constraints} to the directives table and posts a formatted summary to the thread
Conversation context reloads from SQLite after process restart (test: create directive, recreate app object, directive retrievable by thread)
Unit tests with mocked Anthropic + Slack clients cover the idea -> directive -> echo flow
Tests pass
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: DONE 2026-07-08: prompts.py (PM persona: honest reporting, never trades without explicit approval) + conversation.py ConversationCore on judgment_tool_loop with save_directive ToolSpec (persists via StateStore, posts mrkdwn summary in-thread); saved directive reloads from SQLite into the system prompt every call (restart-safe). app.py now routes messages to the core via a MessageResponder protocol. 9 new unit tests (mocked Anthropic FakeClient + recording say). LIVE smoke via onecli: real fable-5 called save_directive, directive persisted + survived simulated restart.

userStories.title: Orchestrator systemd service
userStories.description: As an operator, I want the bot supervised so it survives crashes and reboots.
acceptanceCriteria: ops/rdq-orchestrator.service (systemd user unit) runs the bot under the rdq-orchestrator OneCLI identity with Restart=always
ops/install_services.sh links units into ~/.config/systemd/user/ and daemon-reloads
systemd-analyze verify passes on the unit file
Service started once and observed active (record `systemctl --user status` output in progress notes)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: DONE 2026-07-08: unit + install_services.sh + 10 tests; systemd-analyze verify OK. LIVE-verified active (running), Socket Mode session established under the unit; Restart=always organically proven (recovered a crash loop). Unit sets NO_PROXY=slack.com so Slack bypasses the onecli-injected proxy. Service left INSTALLED BUT STOPPED: SLACK_CHANNEL_ID in .env is a placeholder (C0000000000) — operator must set the real channel id then `systemctl --user enable --now rdq-orchestrator.service`.

userStories.title: FMP EOD bars client
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a typed FMP client so the data pipeline can pull daily bars through the OneCLI proxy.
acceptanceCriteria: data/fmp.py fetches /stable/historical-price-eod/full?symbol=&from=&to= as bare HTTPS (no apikey in code; proxy injects); also wraps splits and dividends endpoints
Handles 429 with retry-after backoff and 401/403 with an actionable missing-secret error message
Unit tests with mocked HTTP cover: date windowing, 429 backoff, 401 message
Live smoke test (marked, skippable offline): AAPL bars for a 1-month window return >15 rows through the proxy
Tests pass
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: DONE 2026-07-08: data/fmp.py FmpClient (EOD bars /historical-price-eod/full with from/to windowing, splits, dividends; all bare HTTPS, proxy injects key). 429 honors Retry-After (exponential fallback), 401/403 raise FmpAuthError naming the onecli identity + setup script. 15 unit tests + live smoke PASSED through the proxy (AAPL May-2026, >15 rows). Live tests self-skip unless RDQ_LIVE_TESTS=1. Rows return ascending; closes are RAW — US-012 adjusts.

userStories.title: Price adjustment factor computation
userStories.description: As a developer, I want split/dividend adjustment factors computed correctly because FMP closes are raw and Qlib needs adjusted prices.
acceptanceCriteria: data/adjust.py computes a per-day adjustment factor series from splits + dividends for a ticker
Unit tests with fixture data: a 10:1 split produces a 0.1 factor step; dividends adjust proportionally; no-event ticker yields factor 1.0 throughout
NVDA 2024-06-10 10:1 split fixture test: adjusted close series shows no >20% single-day cliff across the split date while raw close does
Tests pass
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: DONE 2026-07-08: data/adjust.py adjustment_factors + adjusted_closes — backward adjustment (factor 1.0 on last bar), split 1/ratio step, dividend (prev_close-D)/prev_close proportional, out-of-window events (incl. FMP future ex-dates) ignored. 18 unit tests incl. NVDA 2024-06-10 fixture: raw series cliffs ~90%, adjusted max daily move <20%. Data-quality guards raise AdjustmentError (dup dates, div>=prev close, ratio<=0).

userStories.title: Qlib US data store builder (checkpointed, resumable)
userStories.description: As a developer, I want FMP data converted into a Qlib bin store so research runs on US names.
acceptanceCriteria: data/build_store.py: ticker list -> FMP fetch -> adjusted prices -> Qlib dump_bin format at ~/.qlib/qlib_data/us_data (calendar, instruments, features)
Backfill is checkpointed and resumable: unit test simulates a crash mid-list with mocked fetch and asserts rerun continues without duplicate or missing tickers
Store writes go to a temp dir, validate, then swap (no partial store left on failure) - covered by a unit test
Smoke test: build a 5-ticker store from fixture bars; qlib.init + D.features returns OHLCV for AAPL with no NaN closes
Tests pass
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: DONE 2026-07-08: data/build_store.py — per-ticker JSON checkpoints keyed by (start,end) window (crash resume unit-tested: rerun refetches only the tail, no dup/missing tickers); temp-dir write -> validate_store -> atomic swap via .old (failure paths unit-tested, no partials). Fields open/high/low/close ADJUSTED, volume raw/factor, factor kept. pyqlib 0.9.7 added as a declared dep. Smoke: 5-ticker fixture store read back via qlib.init + D.features, AAPL OHLCV no NaN closes. LIVE-verified through the proxy: 3-ticker 2-month real build, 41 bars/ticker, 0 NaN closes.

userStories.title: Universe generator with liquidity filters
userStories.description: As a developer, I want instrument lists (default us_liquid, sp500, and custom) so strategies rank within a controlled universe.
acceptanceCriteria: data/make_universe.py --name <n> --tickers <list|file> writes a Qlib instruments file into the store
Built-in configs in data/config.yaml: us_liquid (min ADV and min price thresholds) and sp500
Tickers absent from the store are rejected with a printed gap list and nonzero exit
Unit tests on a fixture store cover: filter thresholds, gap rejection, instruments file format
Tests pass
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: DONE 2026-07-08: data/make_universe.py + data/config.yaml (us_liquid: min_adv_usd 20M / min_price $5 / adv_window 20; sp500: committed 504-symbol FMP constituent snapshot data/sp500_tickers.txt). Gap list -> stderr + exit 1. 14 unit tests on fixture stores (thresholds at/below limit, split ticker ADV uses raw dollar volume, gap rejection, file format vs all.txt spans, reserved/invalid names). LIVE-verified on a real 3-ticker proxy-built store; qlib D.instruments(market=<name>) resolves the written file.

userStories.title: Factor source data (daily_pv h5) generation per universe
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the factor coder's source h5 regenerated from the US store for a given universe.
acceptanceCriteria: data/make_factor_source.py generates daily_pv_all.h5 and daily_pv_debug.h5 for a named universe into a target folder (the future FACTOR_CoSTEER_DATA_FOLDER)
Output h5 has MultiIndex (datetime, instrument) with OHLCV columns - asserted by test on the fixture store
Refuses to run for a universe whose instruments file does not exist
Tests pass
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: DONE 2026-07-08: data/make_factor_source.py — universe -> daily_pv_all.h5 + daily_pv_debug.h5 (key='data', MultiIndex (datetime, instrument), $open/$close/$high/$low/$volume/$factor float32) PLUS consumable data_folder/ and data_folder_debug/ subdirs (daily_pv.h5 + README.md) that US-017 points FACTOR_CoSTEER_DATA_FOLDER(_DEBUG) at. Missing universe instruments file -> FactorSourceError, exit 1, nothing written. 12 unit tests incl. qlib D.features parity; LIVE-verified on the real proxy-built store (123 rows, 0 NaN, float32).

userStories.title: US market template copies and prompt overrides
userStories.description: As a developer, I want US-patched copies of RD-Agent's qlib templates so the pinned upstream tree stays pristine.
acceptanceCriteria: research/us_templates/factor_template/ and model_template/ contain copies of the upstream YAMLs with provider_uri -> us_data, region: us, market: us_liquid, S&P500 benchmark, A-share limit_threshold removed, US costs set
research/app_tpl/ holds prompt overrides for any A-share-specific language found in upstream prompts.yaml (grep results documented in docs/decisions.md)
A test renders each template YAML (jinja variables stubbed) and asserts provider_uri/region/market/benchmark values
A test asserts no files under the pinned rdagent install differ from the pinned commit (upstream untouched)
Tests pass
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: DONE 2026-07-08: research/us_templates/{factor,model}_template (5 YAMLs patched: us_data/region us/us_liquid/SPY benchmark/limit_threshold removed/5bps costs+min_cost 0; read_exp_res.py+README byte-identical to upstream, dirs excluded from ruff/pyright) + research/app_tpl partial prompt overrides (2 experiment_setting keys, 3 factor_experiment_loader keys; classify_system_chinese is dead code, not overridden). Grep audit + SPY/cost rationale in docs/decisions.md. 16 tests incl. jinja-stubbed renders, pip-RECORD hash proof upstream untouched, and live APP_TPL resolution through rdagent's load_content (override + fallthrough). GOTCHA for US-017: APP_TPL does NOT redirect workspace template folders (hardcoded); use QLIB_QUANT_* class-path envs. Store must include SPY.

userStories.description: As a developer, I want a single entrypoint that wires env vars and launches a US-market fin_quant run.
acceptanceCriteria: ops/run_us_quant.sh sets QLIB_QUANT_TRAIN_START..TEST_END, FACTOR_CoSTEER_DATA_FOLDER, APP_TPL, template paths, LOG_TRACE_PATH and launches via `onecli run --agent rdq-research -- rdagent fin_quant`
--check mode validates every referenced path/env var exists and exits 0 without launching (verified by running it)
Script supports --loop_n and --all_duration passthrough
Full-run completion criterion documented in header: qlib_res.csv with non-NaN IC, |ARR|<200%, MDD<0 and mlruns/**/pred.pkl indexed by US tickers
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: DONE 2026-07-08: ops/run_us_quant.sh (env wiring incl. dates fanned to QLIB_QUANT_/QLIB_FACTOR_/QLIB_MODEL_ — runners re-read the latter two; FACTOR_CoSTEER_DATA_FOLDER(_DEBUG); APP_TPL; LOG_TRACE_PATH) + research/us_quant.py hook classes (QLIB_QUANT_*_HYPOTHESIS2EXPERIMENT -> convert_response re-injects us_templates; upstream FactorExperiment/ModelExperiment are Experiment aliases so concrete Qlib classes matched). --loop_n/--all_duration passthrough. --check verified live exit 0 against a REAL 31-ticker us_data store (2015..2026-06, SPY included; us_liquid=30 stocks, SPY excluded from universe) + factor source at ~/rdq-data/factor_source/us_liquid. Full multi-hour fin_quant run NOT executed; completion criterion in header.

userStories.title: RD-Agent control-plane systemd service
userStories.description: As a developer, I want rdagent server_ui supervised on localhost so the orchestrator has a control API.
acceptanceCriteria: ops/rdq-research.service runs `rdagent server_ui` bound to 127.0.0.1:19899 via the rdq-research OneCLI identity; NO_PROXY covers 127.0.0.1,localhost and the tailnet range
Unit is NOT exposed via tailscale serve (documented in unit comments; per PLAN.md port table)
systemd-analyze verify passes; service started once and `curl -s http://127.0.0.1:19899/` returns an HTTP response (record in progress notes)
install_services.sh updated to include the unit
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: DONE 2026-07-08: ops/rdq-research.service live and ENABLED (active (running); curl / -> 404 HTTP response, /test -> 200 JSON; ss shows 127.0.0.1:19899 only). KEY FINDING: upstream `rdagent server_ui` main() binds 0.0.0.0 — unit launches research/server_ui.py, a loopback-forced replica of main() (pinned tree unmodified). UI_TRACE_FOLDER/UI_STATIC_PATH -> ~/rdq-runs/server_ui/. NO_PROXY=127.0.0.1,localhost,100.64.0.0/10. Not in tailscale serve (verified live). install_services.sh updated; 24 unit tests incl. subprocess end-to-end bind check.

userStories.title: RD-Agent control client (rdagent_client.py)
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a typed client for server_ui so the orchestrator can start, steer, and inspect research runs.
acceptanceCriteria: orchestrator/rdagent_client.py implements start_run(directive, universe, interaction=True) via POST /trace, pending() via GET /receive, submit(edit) via POST /user_interaction/submit, stop()/resume() via POST /control
Artifact locator resolves a finished loop's qlib_res.csv, ret.pkl, and workspace path from LOG_TRACE_PATH
Unit tests run against a stubbed Flask server exercising every method incl. artifact resolution on a fixture log dir
Tests pass
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: DONE 2026-07-08: orchestrator/rdagent_client.py implemented against the REAL upstream protocol (PRD endpoint sketch was wrong — mapping table in docs/decisions.md): start_run=POST /upload + pre-seeds directive-as-user_instruction and ALPHA20 base features; pending()=POST /trace poll filtered to user_interaction.request (stable .key for US-021 dedup); submit=POST /user_interaction/submit; stop=POST /control; resume maps upstream's 400 to UnsupportedActionError until US-024 extends research/server_ui.py. locate_artifacts() resolves workspace/qlib_res.csv/ret.pkl from 'runner result' pkls. 21 tests vs a threaded Flask stub; live-verified against the running service (health/messages/status/error mapping). WARNING for US-020: rdq-research.service lacks the US env wiring — add it before start_research or server-started runs use CN defaults.

userStories.title: start_research tool wiring Slack thread to run
userStories.description: As an operator, I want 'research X' in a thread to start an RD-Agent run seeded with my directive.
acceptanceCriteria: start_research tool launches a run with the thread's directive as user_instruction and records thread_ts <-> session_path in SQLite runs table
Duplicate start in the same thread is rejected with a message pointing at the active run
Unit tests with stubbed rdagent_client cover: successful start (row written), duplicate rejection
Tests pass
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: DONE 2026-07-08: start_research ToolSpec in ConversationCore — requires a saved directive, launches via RdAgentClient.start_run(directive_instruction, us_liquid), records thread_ts<->session_path (= str(trace_dir)) in runs; duplicate start rejected pointing at the active run (pre-check + DuplicateRunError race guard that stops the orphan run). PREREQ FIXED: rdq-research.service now carries the full US env (dates x3 prefixes, FACTOR_CoSTEER_*, APP_TPL, hook classes, WORKSPACE_PATH, EnvironmentFile=research/.env) — verified in /proc/<pid>/environ after restart. RdAgentClient.trace_id_of() added (inverse of trace_dir) for US-021/024. 9 new tests + LIVE smoke: real fable-5 called the tool, row written, duplicate rejected in-conversation. No real fin_quant run started (multi-hour).

userStories.title: Hypothesis poller with approve/edit/reject buttons
userStories.description: As an operator, I want pending hypotheses posted to my thread with buttons so I can steer the loop without leaving Slack.
acceptanceCriteria: Background poller checks GET /receive for all active runs and posts each pending hypothesis to its owning thread as Block Kit Approve / Edit / Reject buttons
Approve submits the hypothesis unchanged; Edit opens a text round-trip and submits operator text merged into the hypothesis dict; Reject requests a new proposal
Pending interactions persist in SQLite so a restart does not drop them (test: pending row survives poller restart)
Unit tests with stubbed client + mocked Slack cover all three button actions and poller dedup (same hypothesis not posted twice)
Tests pass
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: DONE 2026-07-08: orchestrator/poller.py HypothesisPoller — background thread polls all running runs, posts hypotheses as Block Kit Approve/Edit/Reject (dedup = pending_interactions UNIQUE key; restart-safe, verified). Approve submits the dict unchanged; Edit = in-thread text round-trip (status 'editing', next thread reply merged into hypothesis field — intercepted before the conversation core); Reject submits the dict with a rejection instruction in the hypothesis text (upstream queue protocol has NO regenerate action — docs/decisions.md). Feedback interactions auto-acked or runs would deadlock; FIFO guard: nothing submitted while an earlier hypothesis awaits the operator. 21 unit tests incl. real Bolt block_actions dispatch + end-to-end smoke over real HTTP (StubServerUi + real RdAgentClient + real SQLite): post/dedup/approve/edit-after-restart/reject/feedback all PASS.

userStories.title: Loop-completion summary with metrics and return chart
userStories.description: As an operator, I want a metrics summary and equity curve in the thread when a loop finishes.
acceptanceCriteria: On run completion the poller posts IC, ICIR, Rank IC, ARR, IR, MDD, Sharpe parsed from qlib_res.csv
ret.pkl is rendered to a PNG equity-curve chart (matplotlib, server-side) and uploaded to the thread
Unit test renders the chart from a fixture ret.pkl and asserts a non-empty PNG file; summary formatter tested against a fixture qlib_res.csv
Tests pass
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: DONE 2026-07-08: orchestrator/summary.py (load_metrics/format_summary/compute_sharpe/render_equity_curve) + poller completion detection (status().finished -> summary post + files_upload_v2 chart -> terminal run status LAST, so Slack failures retry; bad artifacts degrade honestly, never retry-loop). GOTCHA: qlib logs NO Sharpe metric — derived from ret.pkl net daily returns (docs/decisions.md); ARR/IR/MDD keys are 1day.excess_return_with_cost.*. 22 new tests + end-to-end smoke over real HTTP (StubServerUi + real RdAgentClient + real locate_artifacts + real matplotlib render): summary+PNG posted, handled exactly once.

userStories.title: set_universe tool for per-run custom universes
userStories.description: As an operator, I want ticker/sector ideas mapped to a confirmed custom universe because RD-Agent(Q) ranks cross-sectionally.
acceptanceCriteria: set_universe resolves an idea to a ticker list (operator-supplied or Claude-proposed) and posts it for in-thread confirmation before any data work
On confirm: validates tickers against the store (gaps reported), calls make_universe + make_factor_source, and renders the run's template copy with market: <custom>
Warns below 30 tickers (configurable) suggesting peer padding; refuses an all-US universe pointing at us_liquid
Universe name + tickers stored with the run row in SQLite
Unit tests cover: gap reporting, min-size warning, all-US refusal, template render with custom market
Tests pass
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: DONE 2026-07-08: orchestrator/universe.py UniverseService + set_universe/confirm_universe ToolSpecs. propose = validation only (all-US superset refusal pointing at us_liquid, reserved-name refusal, <30-ticker warning suggesting peer padding, configurable min_size); posted in-thread, persisted in new universes table (status proposed). confirm = gap check vs store (hard error listing gaps) -> make_universe -> make_factor_source -> template copy rendered with market: <name> under ~/rdq-data/templates/<name>. start_research uses the confirmed universe and stores name+tickers on the run row (runs.universe_tickers, guarded ALTER migration). 27 new tests + LIVE smoke (real fable-5 through proxy, real us_data store): full propose->confirm->start flow PASS. KNOWN GAP (docs/decisions.md): rdq-research.service env still pins factor source/templates to us_liquid — server-spawned runs get the custom universe only via the seeded instruction; per-run env plumbing deferred to the server_ui seam (US-024+).

userStories.notes: DONE 2026-07-09: stop_run/resume_run ToolSpecs in ConversationCore. stop_run = /control stop -> cancel unanswered pending/editing rows ('cancelled') -> run row 'stopped'. resume_run = /control resume from the stored session_path (server extension: research/server_ui.py install_resume_control() wraps the upstream Flask view at runtime — RDLoop.load relaunch under the SAME trace id, END markers stripped from carried-over history) with the saved directive re-seeded (resumed runs re-block on init interactions), then row back to 'running' which re-activates the poller. 17 new tests + live service probes (extension deployed, guards + upstream delegation verified) + real-fable-5 smoke over real HTTP: stop->resume full flow PASS.

userStories.notes: DONE 2026-07-09: orchestrator/notion_client.py NotionClient — create_page (POST /v1/pages), query_db (POST /v1/databases/<id>/query, paginates next_cursor), update_page (PATCH /v1/pages/<id>, properties/archived); Notion-Version: 2022-06-28 on every call, zero Authorization header (source-grep test). Retries: 429 Retry-After, 409/5xx transient exponential; read-after-write = query_db_until(predicate) bounded backoff -> NotionConsistencyError. 20 unit tests + LIVE through proxy: users/me 200 (connector bot), parent page readable, create_page + archive round-trip PASS.

userStories.notes: DONE 2026-07-09: orchestrator/notion_recorder.py NotionRecorder — single Notion write funnel (best-effort: log-and-swallow so outages never break Slack/poller). Directive save -> Research Ideas page (raw idea = first thread message, directive, universe, Status proposed, Thread TS + permalink); start/stop/resume/completion -> Status transitions; posted hypothesis -> Hypothesis Log row (Action pending, Idea relation) with approve/edit/reject/cancel updates; each FEEDBACK auto-ack -> Backtest Results row (metrics via summary.METRIC_SPECS, Sharpe csv-or-ret.pkl, SOTA = feedback decision, workspace, Idea relation). Page-id mappings persist in new notion_pages SQLite table. 22 unit tests (payload asserts on FakeSession) + LIVE smoke against real Notion DBs: all rows landed, status/action/relation verified by read-after-write, smoke pages archived.
